en.extract:
The riverbank warbler (Myiothlypis rivularis), sometimes known as the Neotropical river warbler or just river warbler (leading to confusion with Locustella fluviatilis), is a species of bird in the family Parulidae.
It is found at low levels near water in forests and woodlands. Its range includes three disjunct populations, with one (M. r. mesoleuca) in the eastern Amazon of Brazil, the Guianas, and southern and eastern Venezuela, the second (nominate subspecies) in the Atlantic Forest of south-eastern Brazil, eastern Paraguay and far north-eastern Argentina, and the final population (M. r. bolivianus) in the Yungas of Bolivia.

''Phaeothlypis rivularis'' | range_map = Phaeothlypis rivularis map.svg }} The '''riverbank warbler''' ('''''Myiothlypis rivularis'''''), sometimes known as the Neotropical river warbler or just river warbler (leading to confusion with ''[[river warbler|Locustella fluviatilis]]''), is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Parulidae]]. It is found at low levels near water in forests and woodlands. Its range includes three [[Disjunct distribution|disjunct]] populations, with one (''M. r. mesoleuca'') in the eastern [[Amazon Rainforest|Amazon]] of [[Brazil]], the [[Guianas]], and southern and eastern [[Venezuela]], the second ([[nominate subspecies]]) in the [[Atlantic Forest]] of south-eastern Brazil, eastern [[Paraguay]] and far north-eastern [[Argentina]], and the final population (''M. r. bolivianus'') in the [[Yungas]] of [[Bolivia]]. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Taxonbar|from=Q27075954}} [[Category:Myiothlypis|riverbank warbler]] [[Category:Birds of the Amazon rainforest]] [[Category:Birds of the Guiana Shield]] [[Category:Birds of the Yungas]] [[Category:Birds of the Atlantic Forest]] [[Category:Birds described in 1821|riverbank warbler]] [[Category:Birds of Brazil]] [[Category:Taxonomy articles created by Polbot]] {{Parulidae-stub}}
